reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 389 is a prime number. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. So we need to check if 389 has any divisors other than 1 and 389.

First, check if it's even: 389 is odd, so not divisible by 2. 
Check divisibility by 3: sum of digits 3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3, so no.
Check divisibility by 5: last digit not 0 or 5, so no.
Check divisibility by 7: 7*55=385, remainder 4; 7*56=392, too high. So not divisible by 7.
Check by 11: 11*35=385, remainder 4; 11*36=396, too high. So no.
Check by 13: 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390, too high. So no.
Check by 17: 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391, remainder -2, so no.
Check by 19: 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399, too high. So no.
Check by 23: 23*16=368, remainder 21; 23*17=391, remainder -2, so no.
Check by 29: 29*13=377, remainder 12; 29*14=406, too high. So no.
Check by 31: 31*12=372, remainder 17; 31*13=403, too high. So no.
We only need to check up to sqrt(389). sqrt(389) is about 19.72, so we check primes up to 19. Actually, we already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. So all primes up to 19: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide 389. Therefore 389 is prime.

Answer: yes.
